Hack Beyond Limits – Rathinam College
Won the 24-hour hackathon, earning ₹30,000 worth of gift vouchers for their problem-solving skills.
Kanam 2025 – Runners-up
Thamilarasan G P, Majid Husain J, and Tharan A (2nd-year IT) secured Runners-up at Kanam 2025 Project Expo hosted by Dr. NGP Institute of Technology.
i-Cube Hackathon – Special Mention Award
Rajat Jaiswal, Sri Ram, Sridharan, and Shasty Venkatesh (2nd-year AI&DS) won a Special Mention Award at the i-Cube Hackathon for their innovative green-tech project.
Hack the Future – Silver Winners
SanJai L (IV-AI&DS) and Abinaya S (IV-CSE) won Silver at the Hack the Future hackathon by Devpost, earning $650 (~₹55,000) and $1,000 in Patreon credits.
Digital India RISC-V Symposium 2025 – IIT Madras
Secured Second Place with a cash prize of ₹35,000 at IIT Madras, showcasing excellence in cutting-edge technology.
AIQUBIT Tech Fest 2K25 – Karpagam Academy
Winners of the prestigious AIQUBIT competition, proving their expertise in IT and innovation.
VISAI 2025 – Vel Tech University
Achieved 1st, 2nd & 3rd positions in technical and non-technical events at Vel Tech University under sustainability initiatives.
Health-Hack at VIT Bhopal
Secured 4th place in a competition featuring over 300+ teams, in collaboration with Johns Hopkins University.
Maker’s Arena – 24-Hour Hardware Hackathon
Innovators behind a sustainable shore energy project, winning ₹3 lakhs in funding from StartupTN.
MESCIA 2K25 – Paper Presentation
Winners of the competition at Kongu Engineering College, bagging a cash prize of ₹1,500.
National Science Day Project Exhibition 2025
Secured 1st Prize at the National Radio Astronomy Center, Ooty, for an outstanding project exhibition.
Paper Presentation at KIT
3rd Runner-Up at Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology for an impactful research presentation.
Paper Presentation at Hindustan College
Secured First Place for an innovative research paper on cybersecurity.
SheLeads – Idea Pitch
Achieved 4th place in the idea pitch competition at the 50th Park Institution of Innovation.
Unplugged 2.0 – 24-Hour Hardware Hackathon
Secured 3rd place with a cash prize of ₹20,000, showcasing innovation and technical prowess.
Yugam 2025 – Think, Solve, and Win
Secured 2nd prize in a strategic problem-solving contest at Kumaraguru College.
DAKSH AI Hackathon at SASTRA University
Winner of the AI-based hackathon at SASTRA, earning a ₹20,000 cash award for technological innovation.
Udayam 25 Paper Presentation
Winners of the paper presentation competition at KIT, earning a ₹3,000 cash prize for their research excellence.
KEC Kackathon 25
Runner-up in the 30-hour hackathon at Kongu Engineering College, receiving ₹5,000 in cash for their innovative EduTech solutions.
Anveshan 2024 – Analog Devices Panel Choice Award
Secured the prestigious Panel Choice Award at Anveshan 2024, winning ₹50,000 in cash for their outstanding innovation alongside IITs and NITs.

Celebrating Excellence in Sustainability - IGEN Awards 2024
Santhosh E.M. and Dhanusuya K. (III Year CSBS) received the Youth Iconic Award at the IGEN Sustainability Champion Awards 2024, held at Anna University, Chennai. The event honored contributions to sustainable development.

Under The EK Bharat Shreshtha Bharat (EBSB) program by NITTTR Chennai, hosted by IIM Jammu.
Avishkaar Season 2 National level 48Hrs  Hackathon by Geeks for Geeks
Third-year ECE students Pavan Khalyan, Hariharan, and Vigneshkumar triumphed in AVISHKAAR Season 2, a national-level 48-hour hackathon by Geeks for Geeks, securing a cash prize of ₹1.25 lakhs.
The CATERPILLAR Autonomy Challenge @IIT Madras
Rajkumar N S, Nitish M, Saravanakumar G, Angelin Sharmell E, and Sivasathya S have been shortlisted for the finals of The Caterpillar Autonomy Challenge at IIT Madras, demonstrating exceptional innovation and teamwork.

PEC Hacks 2.0
Team Technitiz, comprising Aswath CM, Aadhavan P, Muruga Perumal R, Cibiya N, and Nithin Kumar, claimed victory at PEC Hacks 2.0, winning a remarkable ₹1 lakh cash prize with their innovative solutions.
International Collegiate Programming Contest (ICPC) Chennai Regional 2024
Sakthi Narayanan R, Pagalavan M, and Naveen Kumar S secured 10th in Tamil Nadu & 90th in India, continuing SECE’s streak of excellence.
